title: Verhoeff’s Iron Hematoxylin Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cjrcn","text":"This protocol is used to stain elastic tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8erg0","text":"This results in elastic fibers and nuclei stained black.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dl105","text":"Staining of elasin allows for quantification of elastin breaks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dsiql","text":"Place slides in glass holder. Be sure that slides are labeled appropriately.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ds4un","text":"Prepare reagents before starting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Acetone to fix If slides keep falling off slide, air dry for 30 mintues before fixation

Ferric chloride (2% wt/vol) Water 3 washes Check slide for differention at 1m30s and for every 30s afterward * Halt staining when nuclei and elastin fibers are still black with weak background. *** If over-differentiated, go back to the step 3.
creator: Jeff Z Chen
